beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and grab coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Jackson woke early yesterday for sunrise; today he's still riding that energy but it's drizzly so no early run. Quick morning routine before heading out.

beats.activity: Commute to SoDo office, driving south on surface streets to avoid SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Jackson avoids it by leaving early and taking I-5 south. Risk-averse personality means he plans around known closures.

beats.activity: Deep work on design iterations for current project; catch up on Slack and team standup.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday was high-energy and social (board games, crowded sports bar); today is recovery mode. Steady rain outside makes indoor focus natural. Risk-averse tendency means powering through routine work.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby casual spot; grabs a sandwich and soup to eat indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain continues until 2pm; Jackson stays near the office rather than venturing out. Quick meal keeps him fueled without wasting time.

beats.activity: Afternoon design work, prototype testing, and design review with team.
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Jackson stays focused on work through late afternoon. Yesterday's late-night board games mean he's probably not pushing for extra hours today.

beats.activity: Commute home via I-5 north, surface streets through Greenwood.
beats.reasoning: Standard evening commute back to Greenwood. Leaves at reasonable hour (energy was low yesterday, so no late-night push today).

beats.activity: Quick grocery run to pick up ingredients for dinner and snacks.
beats.reasoning: Jackson is meticulous with expenses and meal planning. Rain has cleared; quick errand in his neighborhood before settling in for the evening.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home; review expense spreadsheet while eating.
beats.reasoning: Low energy day after yesterday's late social event. Jackson cooks at home and catches up on his meticulous expense tracking—a grounding routine.

beats.activity: Wind down with a board game solo playthrough and some casual reading; relax before bed.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's board game night was energizing but exhausting. Tonight is quiet, introspective leisure at home—Jackson recharges alone before sleep.
Recovery sleep after yesterday's late night. Winds down naturally and gets a full 7.75 hours to reset for Wednesday.

diary:
Woke up to grey skies and that particular Seattle drizzle that makes everything feel slower. The SR-520 closure meant I had to snake through SoDo on surface streets—added maybe ten minutes, but honestly I didn't mind. Gave me time to think before diving into work.

Morning was heads-down design iterations. The new prototype is coming together, though there's this one interaction that's still not quite right. Grabbed a sandwich at Salumi around lunch; ate inside watching the rain streak the windows. Afternoon felt productive—design review went smooth, team seemed aligned on the direction.

Home by three-thirty. Whole Foods run was quick, just grabbed what I needed for dinner. Made pasta, ate at the kitchen counter while reviewing my expenses—thrilling stuff, I know. By evening the rain had stopped and I felt that nice tired, so I played through a solo board game and read for a bit. Good day. Quiet day. Exactly what I needed.
thought_process: The weather and traffic closure shaped my whole rhythm today. Instead of fighting the bridge closure, I leaned into the surface street route—seemed smarter than joining the chaos. Work felt focused because I wasn't stressed about commute logistics. I chose to eat at Salumi rather than my desk because the weather made me want to be around people, even if just sitting near them. By afternoon I was in flow, so the design review landed well. The evening felt like a natural wind-down; I wasn't tempted to work late because the day had genuine momentum and closure. Small choices—the grocery run, the solo game, the expense review—felt grounding after a creative morning. Rainy days usually make me introspective, and today that translated into being present with whatever I was doing rather than scattered.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
